news.text: Indian shares log best week in over five years as Iran ceasefire eases investor anxiety - Reuters
Shares gain before US-Iran talks; oil edges higher - Reuters
Strait still shut and Lebanon fighting strains truce as US and Iran aim for first talks - Reuters
World Food Programme warns Lebanon facing food security crisis due to Iran war - Reuters
Ceasefire sends dollar on weekly drop; US-Iran talks in focus - Reuters
Britain's Tesco to shine light on inflation risks from Iran war - Reuters

brief:
◆ Market Overview

Markets are catching a relief bid pre-market with SPY up 0.58% to $679.91 as Iran ceasefire talks calm geopolitical nerves. VIX is bleeding out 4% to 30.39, but still elevated above its 20 comfort zone, indicating fragile sentiment beneath the surface optimism.

◆ What to Watch

  • US-Iran Diplomatic Talks - First direct negotiations could either extend this relief rally or spark fresh volatility if talks break down
  • Oil Price Action - Energy sector down 1.24% despite crude edging higher; watch for disconnect resolution
  • Consumer Inflation Data - March CPI expected to surge due to Iran war impact on commodities and supply chains
  • End-of-Week Position Squaring - Light economic calendar means focus on technical levels and risk-off positioning

◆ Sector Rotation

Consumer Discretionary leading the charge up 1.73% as ceasefire hopes fuel risk-on appetite, while Energy paradoxically sells off despite oil strength. Defensive plays like Utilities and Staples gaining ground suggests investors still want hedges despite the relief rally.
✦ Bull Case
Ceasefire momentum could trigger significant short covering rally with VIX collapse toward 20 level, especially if US-Iran talks show progress
⚠ Bear Case
VIX still above 30 signals underlying stress; any diplomatic setback could spark violent reversal with leveraged longs trapped
⚡ Key Driver
Dollar weakness from ceasefire optimism could fuel commodity and growth stock rotation if sustained through weekend

◆ Levels That Matter

  • SPY 680 - Round number resistance; break above targets 685 gap fill from last week's selloff
  • QQQ 615 - Key resistance level that's held multiple times; breakout would signal tech leadership return
  • VIX 28 - Critical support; break below accelerates volatility crush and fuels rally extension
  • XLE 85 - Energy sector support; violation signals broader commodity complex weakness despite oil strength

Not a prediction. A preparation. Make your plan before the bell.
